sections.paragraph:
fut. יֶחְשַׁךְ to be dark, to be darkened, to be surrounded with darkness. (Syr. ܚܫܶܟ id.), used of the light of the sun, Job 18:6 Isa 5:30 13:10 of the earth, Exo 10:15 of eyes becoming dim, Lam 5:17 Psa 69:24 of men, Ecc 12:3.

Hiphil-

(1) to darken, to make dark. Amo 5:8, יֹום לַיְלָה הֶחֱשִׁיךְ “he makes the day dark (even unto) night;” followed by לְ 8:9 . Metaphorically Job 38:2, מִי זֶה מַחְשִׁיךְ עֵצָה וגו׳ “who is this, who darkens (my) counsel with unwise words;” i.e. strives to hinder it.

(2) intrans. to be dark (properly to make darkness), Psa 139:12 Jer 13:16.

[Derivatives, חֲשֹׁוךְ and the following words.]
